management_guidance.context: Q2 FY2026 earnings call, raised low end
Raised by 10 bps from prior guidance
Reaffirmed on Q4 FY2026 earnings call
Q1 FY2027 earnings call guidance, ~10% constant currency growth

competitive_position.market_share: 20.7% of global CRM market in 2026 (per KoalaGains / technologychecker.io)
competitive_position.moat: Comprehensive platform with strong integration ecosystem; used by >80% of Fortune 500 companies.
competitive_position.threats: Competitors focus on cost, speed, and specific use cases, potentially eroding market share.

key_facts.fact: Agentforce and Data 360 ARR reached $2.9 billion in FY2026, up more than 200% YoY, including $1.1 billion from Informatica Cloud.
key_facts.source: FY2026 10-K / Q4 FY2026 earnings call transcript
key_facts.caveat: ARR is annualized recurring revenue, not recognized revenue.

key_facts.fact: FY2026 total revenue was $41.525 billion (per EDGAR XBRL), up 9.6% YoY from $37.895 billion in FY2025.
Q1 FY2027 revenue (period ending 2026-04-30) was $11.133 billion, beating analyst estimates of $11.05 billion.
Q1 FY2027 adjusted EPS was $3.88 vs consensus $3.13, a 24.1% beat.
In March 2026, Salesforce funded a $25 billion accelerated share repurchase by issuing senior notes and a $6 billion term loan.
Salesforce guided FY30 revenue target of $63 billion (11% CAGR).

analyst_thesis.bull_case: Agentforce and Data 360 ARR surged to $2.9B (+200% YoY), indicating strong AI-driven growth. Management raised FY30 revenue target to $63B (11% CAGR). Analyst consensus target of $257.03 implies 34.5% upside from current price of $191.10.
analyst_thesis.bear_case: Stock declined 25-30% YTD in 2026 amid AI concerns, with forward P/E of 12.3x reflecting skepticism. Debt-funded $25B buyback increases leverage, and 8.6% annual revenue growth forecast may not sustain premium multiple.

key_institutions.thesis: 34.5% upside potential based on analyst target price.
Analysts expect 9.5% earnings and 8.6% revenue CAGR through 2026.

data_quality.notes: Most segment revenue data from third-party (Backlinko), not official segment reporting. EDGAR XBRL lacks segment detail. Guidance and key facts from earnings calls and SEC filings are high confidence.
